provisions.title: BY VIRTUE OOF THE POWER VESTED IN THE MILLITARY
provisions.content:
By vintue of the powers vested ;n the Military Government
    .of Santo Domingo, the following Order is dictated and promul-
e   fated :
         1. Authority is hereby conferred upon
              the Contador General de Hacienda;
              the Director Genwal de RenW I n t e r n s ;
              each Colector de Rentas Internas;
              each Inspector de Rentas Internas ;
              each Agente Especial de Eentas Internas; and
              each Tasador del-Depantamento de Impuesta
                   sobre la Propiedad,
    to administer oaths in any case in which such formality may be
    necessary or appropriate in the exercise of their official duties.
    This authority to administer oaths is intended and shall be under-
    stood to cover all matter connected directly or indirectly with the
    administration of 'any of the several laws relating t o
         (a) the assessment and collection of taxes, duties, or
               revenues of whatever nature due the Dominican Gov-
               ernment;
         (b) the custody and disbursement of funds of t h e Domin-
               ican Government ;
0        (c) the printing, custody, sale and use of Internal Re-
               venue and postage stampe of the Dominican Govern-
               ment ;

    (d)    the purchases, custody, use and disposition o i ma-
                         ,.
                  , rnp:xcs a d crcperty belonging t o the Domin-            e
           ican Government, including lands and buildings and
           titles thereto; and
     (e)   the accounts and records relating to any of the above
           ,mentioned matters.
     2     dr the purpose of investigating frauds attemnted or
perpetrated to the prejudice of the Dominican Govrnment, t h e
officers above mentioned ase authorized to require the att-,n dance
of and examine vi!tT:cssez under oath 2nd i o require the present-
ation of documentary evidence, whether contained in public or
private documents or other real evidence when pertinent to the
matter under investigation.
     3 . It shall be t h e duty of every person in the Dominican
Republic to appear when summoned by the officers above
mentioned, make declarations under oath, answer, the questions
propounded, and present the documents or evidence called for,
and any person who, when so required shall refuse to do any
or all of t h e acts above mentioned shall be denounced by such
officer to any judge, tribunal or court having jurisdiction,
whereupon i t shall become the duty of such judge, tribunal 3r
                                                                         *
court, as the case may be, t o issue an order in a proper case in
the form provided f o r in the Law of Criminal Procedure and
subsequent m,odifizations thereof, t o compel the witness to attend
and testif:-, t3 compel the proJuction of th2 documents of evidence
called for or a search of pr.?mises to discover the> r:' it shou!d
be necessary. Payment or advance of fces and expenses of
witnesses summoned by courts in this manner shall be made
as prescribed by Law.
     Witnesses obeying the summons of the officers above men.-
tioned shall receive the same fees and expenses as provided for
in the Tarifa de Costas Judiciales as now in force. Payment
thereof shall be made in the manner prescribed by the Orden
Ejecutiva No. 302. c r r ~ c p tthc olic'nei. of the witness certified
                                  T~

t o be correct by the Executive Order.
     5 . Any person who shall n a k e m y false statement under
oath before any of the officers mentioned in Paragraph 1 hereof
shall be guilty of periury, a i d shd!, upop conviction before
court having criminal jurisdictim, be punished as provided in
Executive Order No. 202, dated August 28, 1918.
                                                                         0
    6 . No fee shal! be collected by any of the Officers above
mentioned for administering an oath or for legalizing a sWOCn

     declaration, but any such declaration when made voluntarily and
     not for the exclusive benefit of the Dominican Govenunent shall
*    be subject t o the payment of the corresponding internal revenue
     tax on documents unIess specifically ,exempted therefrom by the
     terms of Executive Order No. 197. dated August 19, 1918.
         7. Nothing contsinsl in this Order shall be construed to
     deprive any person of the right not to be compelled t o testify
     against himself.
         8. This Order shall become effective on and after the
     date of its publication and any Laws or parts of Laws in conflict
     with the terms hereof, are hereby revoked.

                                       THOMAS SNOWDEN,
                                     Rear-Admiral, U . S. Navy.
                               Military Governor of Smto Domingo.
     Santo Domingo City, D. R . ,
         7 January, 1920.
